figures.caption: Table 1: Quantitative comparisons. Bold: Best. Underline: Second Best. Our method consistently outperforms previous solutions on both VBench and Q-Align

Fig. 4: Qualitative comparisons of novel view video synthesis. Our method generates high-fidelity, plausible embodied manipulation videos, outperforming baseline approaches in both structural consistency and visual quality.
Fig. 10: Visualization results of compositional 4D embodied data, featuring 4D-consistent sequences with diverse robotic arm foregrounds and varied backgrounds.

Fig. 1: Introducing 4D World Model. Multiview information is crucial for embodied manipulation and planning, and there is an urgent need for embodied multiview 4D world models to provide comprehensive spatial environmental representations for downstream tasks.
Table 3: Real-world embodied experiments on FR3 with π0.5 Droid. Each task has 10 trials with randomized object positions. Tasks: T1-T3 (seen), T4-T5 (unseen).

Fig. 11: Visualization of interaction motion priors. Showcasing three different foreground acquisition strategies: direct acquisition from compositional data, optical flow estimation, and video object segmentation.

Fig. 6: Ablation on the compositional training data. Comparison between our full model and a variant trained without compositional data. The yellow boxes show that our strategy is key to mitigating geometric distortions and ensuring generalization across diverse robotic morphologies.

Fig. 3: Interaction-Aware Block. This module projects Q,K, V and interaction biases via linear layers. The bias, derived from foreground masks, is injected into the guided path to prioritize manipulation regions, ensuring geometric consistency across viewpoint changes (qualitative comparison in heatmaps).

Fig. 2: Overview of Embody4D. We construct paired embodied training videos in 3D via compositional synthesis and process them with a “warp-then-inpaint” architecture. The source video is reconstructed into a point cloud and projected to the target view to produce warped RGB plus occupancy masks; these are concatenated and passed to a confidence module that adaptively injects different noise levels. Finally, a backbone model with an interaction-aware block outputs the target-view video with highly consistent manipulation details.
Table 4: Real-world embodied experiments on FR3 with π0.5 Droid. Each task has 10 trials with randomized object positions. Tasks: T1-T3 (seen), T4-T5 (unseen).
Fig. 5: Qualitative comparisons of the ablations. Results of ablation on embodied interaction-aware attention and confidence-aware adaptive noise injection strategy.
Fig. 15: Qualitative visualization results of our method and the baselines.
Fig. 8: Comparison of successful novel-view synthesis counts under four experimental settings on seen tasks and OOD unseen tasks
Fig. 9: Visualization results of real-world embodied experiments.
